candidate_problems.text: How can 'integrated invariants' be defined non-circularly and measured in a way that distinguishes phenomenal consciousness from merely sophisticated control or representation? The conjecture depends on a mathematically precise notion of invariant-preserving state-space organization, but it remains open what the invariants are, how to individuate them across architectures, and how to avoid redescribing consciousness in structural terms without explanatory gain.

Why should preservation of this specific structural regime generate phenomenality at all, rather than only functional unity, self-monitoring, or behavioral coherence? The central unresolved tension is the explanatory gap between identifying a narrow equivalence class of informational dynamics and showing why membership in that class entails subjective experience rather than just complex cognition.

What empirical or computational interventions could cleanly dissociate the conjecture from rival theories such as global workspace, higher-order thought, predictive processing, or IIT? The proposal is promising as a structural synthesis, but it is still unclear what decisive experiments, lesion-style manipulations, or artificial-system constructions would uniquely confirm or falsify the role of invariant-preserving topology rather than neighboring notions of integration and recurrence.
